lastSnapshot.handoverInstructions:
1. Conduct frequent online research to refine and enhance all approaches, using up-to-date best practices for LLM/agent systems, TypeScript, ESM, and plugin architectures.
  2. Always adhere to the established project guidelines: strict type-safety, ESM-only, plugin-based extensibility, self-describing APIs, dynamic capability discovery, and robust CI quality enforcement.
  3. Prioritize and execute all remaining tasks and refactors in the backlog, including DRY/YAGNI compliance, dead code removal, doc/code sync, and advanced agent orchestration features.
  4. Implement, monitor, and continuously improve advanced circuitbreaker logic for all agent orchestration, including retries, timeouts, fallback, escalation, and health checks. Use opossum or equivalent, and emit telemetry for all circuitbreaker state changes.
  5. Keep all documentation, capability registries, and describe() outputs up to date and in sync with the codebase.
  6. If context is lost, reload the latest context-snapshot.json and agentBacklog.json to recover the current state and roadmap.
  7. All outputs and APIs must remain maximally LLM/agent-usable, machine-readable, and context-window efficient.
  8. If in doubt, consult the latest online research and update the approach accordingly.

planned.rationale: Enable agents to auto-generate code mutations, patch files, and optionally create PRs for schema drift, TODOs, and refactor suggestions.
planned.notes: Integrate with git, create .patch files, and support auto-branch/commit if enabled.

planned.rationale: Allow agents to register custom extractors, analyzers, or output formats for new languages/frameworks.
planned.notes: Support plugin discovery and dynamic loading.

dream.description: Agents register their capabilities, specialties, and working style. The context engine recommends which agent should handle a given mutation or plan.
Agents can negotiate, delegate, and coordinate complex refactors or feature builds, with context engine as the mediator.

dream.feature: Live Code Mutation & PR Creation (Fully Automated)
dream.description: Agents can auto-create PRs, run tests, and self-merge if all checks pass.
Agents can search the entire codebase, docs, and message history by meaning, using embeddings and a local or remote vector DB.
Agents rate the usefulness of context fields, and the engine tunes its prioritization and compression strategies automatically.
Agents can send structured negotiation messages, propose plans, and reach consensus on code changes.

backlog.task: Scan for large/complex functions and split as needed
Remove dead code flagged by Knip
Review and resolve all TODO/FIXME comments
Ensure all exports are clean and non-duplicated
Add a 'doctor' or 'self-diagnose' CLI command for ESM/TS/config issues
Integrate dead code detection (Knip) into the CLI with actionable output
Centralize error reporting with debug hints
Provide a CLI utility for test output normalization
Add a doc/code sync checker for describe() and CLI help
Expose a refactor suggestion endpoint (static analysis)

circuitbreakerRecommendations.summary: Use robust circuitbreaker patterns (e.g., opossum) for all agent orchestration, tool/plugin calls, and LLM invocations. Always wrap external/service calls in a circuitbreaker with sensible defaults (timeouts, error thresholds, fallback, health checks). Emit telemetry for breaker state changes and escalate to supervisor/monitor agents on repeated failure. Support dynamic tuning and hot-reload of breaker configs.
bestPractices: Wrap all network, LLM, and plugin/tool calls in a circuitbreaker.
Set timeouts and error thresholds appropriate to the operation.
Emit telemetry events for breaker open/close/halfOpen states.
Provide fallback logic and escalate to supervisor/monitor agents on repeated failure.
Allow dynamic tuning of breaker configs (env/config/CLI).
Document all circuitbreaker usage and configs in describe() and docs.

contextCompressionSuggestion: Trim semanticIndex to most frequent 100 keywords.
